news_items.title: Nagelsmann confirms duo will start against Ecuador – Undav on the bench again
news_items.text:
Nagelsmann confirms duo will start against Ecuador – Undav on the bench again

FCB B04 SGE BVB SCF M05 RBL SVW VFB BMG WOL FCA FCU FCSP TSG FCH KOE HSV

World: World Cup

MetLife Stadium (East Rutherford, New Jersey)

Ecuador

Germany

Lineups Standing

Julian Nagelsmann is planning to make just two changes for Germany's game against Ecuador, with David Raum and Antonio Rüdiger set to start. Despite reports suggesting otherwise, the in-form Deniz Undav will begin on the bench again.

| Photo by Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images |
| --- |

Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann will keep changes to his starting lineup to a minimum for his side's final World Cup group-stage game against Ecuador.

With Germany already certain to finish top of Group E heading into the match, there had been speculation that Nagelsmann could opt for heavy rotation.

However, the 38-year-old announced at his pre-match press conference that he has decided to stick with most of the starting lineup from Germany's first two games in order to maintain rhythm.

"It's the most legitimate question of all to ask whether we should make changes or not. The coaching staff spent a lot of time discussing it and talking to th

news_items.title: Ecuador vs. Germany preview: Team news and predicted lineups – World Cup 2026
news_items.text:
Ecuador vs. Germany preview: Team news and predicted lineups – World Cup 2026

FCB B04 SGE BVB SCF M05 RBL SVW VFB BMG WOL FCA FCU FCSP TSG FCH KOE HSV

World: World Cup

MetLife Stadium (East Rutherford, New Jersey)

Ecuador

Germany

Lineups Standing

Here is our preview of Germany's third game at the 2026 World Cup against Ecuador.

| Photo by Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images |
| --- |

Having already secured top spot in Group E with victories over Curacao (7-1) and Cote d'Ivoire (2-1), Germany will wrap up the group stage against Ecuador at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford on Thursday (22:00 CEST).

Ecuador, meanwhile, need a victory to secure a place in the round of 32 after losing to Cote d'Ivoire and most recently being held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Curacao. Despite registering 27 shots, Sebastián Beccacece's side failed to score and now face a must-win encounter.

Although Germany's place in the knockout stages is already guaranteed, midfielder Nadiem Amiri insists there will be no easing off.

"We need to keep this momentum going. Winning breeds confidence. We owe it to the other sides to approach the game as though our own tournament depends on it. It’s still a ve

news_items.text:
Ecuador vs Germany Preview, Lineups & Team News | RotoWire

# Ecuador vs Germany Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group E

Ecuador vs Germany clash in East Rutherford for their decisive 2026 World Cup Group E finale. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

Updated on June 25, 2026 7:48AM EST

Share

By:

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

Updated on June 25, 2026 7:48AM EST

# Ecuador vs Germany Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group E

Group E comes down to this, and the two teams want very different things from it. Germany are already through on six points and are really just protecting the top seed, while Ecuador sit on a single point and have to beat the best side in the group to have any chance of going with them. That is a tall order against a team that has scored nine goals in two games at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford.

There is little romance to be found h

news_items.title: Germany lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for final World Cup 2026 Group E clash - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Germany lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for final World Cup 2026 Group E clash - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 3

New York New Jersey Stadium

# Germany lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for final World Cup 2026 Group E clash

By Oliver Thomas| 23 Jun 2026 19:05, Last updated: 23 Jun 2026 20:14

© Imago / Kirchner-Media

Germany head coach Julian Nagelsmann is required to make at least one change to his starting lineup for Thursday’s 2026 World Cup Group E clash against Ecuador.

Borussia Dortmund centre-back Nico Schlotterbeck has been ruled out for the rest of the tournament with an ankle injury sustained in the 2-1 comeback victory over Ivory Coast last weekend.

Antonio Rudiger is the most likely candidate to deputise at the heart of the defence alongside Jonathan Tah, while Waldemar Anton and Malick Thiaw represent alternative options for Nagelsmann.

With Germany having already qualified for the knockout rounds as group winners, Nagelsmann may consider making changes in several other positions to keep his team fresh.

Oliver Baumann was set to be Germany’s No.1 goalkeeper before 40-year-old Manuel Neuer opted to come out of retirement. The Hoffenheim shot-stopper will

Julian Nagelsmann is planning to make just two changes for Germany's game against Ecuador, with David Raum and Antonio Rüdiger set to start. Despite reports suggesting otherwise, the in-form Deniz Undav will begin on the bench again.

| Photo by Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images |
| --- |

Germany coach Julian Nagelsmann will keep changes to his starting lineup to a minimum for his side's final World Cup group-stage game against Ecuador.

With Germany already certain to finish top of Group E heading into the match, there had been speculation that Nagelsmann could opt for heavy rotation.

However, the 38-year-old announced at his pre-match press conference that he has decided to stick with most of the starting lineup from Germany's first two games in order to maintain rhythm.

"It's the most legitimate question of all to ask whether we should make changes or not. The coaching staff spent a lot of time discussing it and talking to th"}

Here is our preview of Germany's third game at the 2026 World Cup against Ecuador.

| Photo by Alexander Hassenstein/Getty Images |
| --- |

Having already secured top spot in Group E with victories over Curacao (7-1) and Cote d'Ivoire (2-1), Germany will wrap up the group stage against Ecuador at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford on Thursday (22:00 CEST).

Ecuador, meanwhile, need a victory to secure a place in the round of 32 after losing to Cote d'Ivoire and most recently being held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by Curacao. Despite registering 27 shots, Sebastián Beccacece's side failed to score and now face a must-win encounter.

Although Germany's place in the knockout stages is already guaranteed, midfielder Nadiem Amiri insists there will be no easing off.

# Ecuador vs Germany Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group E

Group E comes down to this, and the two teams want very different things from it. # Germany lineup vs. Ecuador: Predicted XI for final World Cup 2026 Group E clash

By Oliver Thomas| 23 Jun 2026 19:05, Last updated: 23 Jun 2026 20:14

© Imago / Kirchner-Media

Germany head coach Julian Nagelsmann is required to make at least one change to his starting lineup for Thursday’s 2026 World Cup Group E clash against Ecuador.

Borussia Dortmund centre-back Nico Schlotterbeck has been ruled out for the rest of the tournament with an ankle injury sustained in the 2-1 comeback victory over Ivory Coast last weekend.

Antonio Rudiger is the most likely candidate to deputise at the heart of the defence alongside Jonathan Tah, while Waldemar Anton and Malick Thiaw represent alternative options for Nagelsmann.

With Germany having already qualified for the knockout rounds as group winners, Nagelsmann may consider making changes in several other positions to keep his team fresh.
